name: core
description: v2.18.0 (2026-05-03) — Opt-in telemetry push restored; default OFF posture preserved. Founder direction "make it on if the user said yes". The --telemetry on flag added in v2.9.1 persisted telemetry_optin=true to .claude/sutra-project.json, but scripts/push.sh:19-24 had a hard gate from the v2.0 privacy reset that blocked all pushes regardless of consent. Net effect: opt-in flag was theater — no transport. v2.18.0 lifts the gate. SUTRA_TELEMETRY=0 short-circuits BEFORE the telemetry_optin check (uniform with capture path) — kill-switch now stops both capture AND push. python3 JSON probes in push.sh / go.sh / status.sh / flush-telemetry.sh OPTIN-probe replaced with jq (matches start.sh v2.13.0 EDR-killed-python3 fix); silent toggle/push failure on broken-python3 hosts closed. SUTRA_TELEMETRY=0 early exits added to hooks/posttool-counter.sh and hooks/emit-metric.sh so the kill-switch covers the capture rail too (PRIVACY.md amendment claims uniform coverage; needed both rails to deliver). PRIVACY.md amendment explicitly supersedes v2.0 "no outbound transmission" sentence + tier-default T1/T2 paragraph; discloses on opt-in: WHAT pushed (telemetry rows + manifest with install_id/project_id/project_name_optional/sutra_version/push_count/first_seen/last_seen), CADENCE (every Stop), DESTINATION (collaborator-visible sankalpasawa/sutra-data per PROTO-024 V1), KILL-SWITCH (SUTRA_TELEMETRY=0 uniform). Banner in _sutra_project_lib.sh branches on 3 telemetry states (kill-switched / opt-in ENABLED / off). New SUTRA_DATA_REMOTE env override on push.sh for testability + self-host. Test-onboard-to-push rewritten: calls push.sh directly, asserts opt-in triggers push / SUTRA_TELEMETRY=0 skips / jq absent → exit 127 / opt-in=false skips. Codex review chain R1-R5 converged at PASS — verdict file at .enforcement/codex-reviews/2026-05-03-v2.18.0-opt-in-push.md (DIRECTIVE-ID 1777800873). + v2.17.0 (2026-05-01) — Connector tools and routine project edits no longer prompt every time. Two coverage gaps in the existing permission system are closed: (1) MCP connector tools — Slack/Apollo/Atlassian/Gmail/Drive/Calendar/HubSpot/Read.ai/Playwright auto-approve on read-class verbs (search/list/get/read/fetch/query/describe/enrich/match/status/info/view/metadata/count/index/profile/resolve/open/outline/availability/preview/download); explicit per-vendor mutator/send denylist always prompts (Slack send-message/draft/schedule/canvas, Gmail create-draft/send/forward/delete/archive/label-mutations, Drive create/copy/batch-update/import, Calendar create/update/delete/respond, Apollo create/update/campaigns, Atlassian create/edit/transition/comment/worklog, HubSpot manage-crm/feedback, Playwright stateful click/fill-form/navigate/run-code-unsafe). (2) First-time Edit/Write inside cwd — routine project edits no longer prompt; gated by prompt-list (.env*/.git/.npmrc/.pypirc/credentials/secrets/CI/deploy/k8s/helm/.terraform/Pulumi/wrangler/railway/firebase/cloudbuild/app.yaml/supabase/cross-cwd). Founder direction D44. Sister to D43 (ADR-002 OUT-DIRECT 3-check) — same friction class, different actor (harness vs model). Codex caught architectural misframing in R1 — PERMISSIONS.md is a sibling discipline to HUMAN-SUTRA-LAYER.md, not a sub-form (Stage-3 owns model emission, not harness dialogs). ADR-001 3-direction MECE invariant preserved. New lib/mcp_trust_mode.py module (~190 LOC, separate from sh_trust_mode.py to keep shell vs MCP semantics decoupled). Tier 1.7 (MCP) + Tier 1.8 (first-time-edit) added to PERMISSIONS.md. Telemetry: tool_class + decision_basis fields added to .enforcement/permission-gate.jsonl. Threat-model honesty: widening Edit/Write IS a deliberate policy expansion (codex P2 fold). Codex R1 CHANGES-REQUIRED (2 P1 / 2 P2 / 2 P3) → R2 ADVISORY (4 tighten-before-ship folded into implementation), DIRECTIVE-ID 1777641500. + v2.16.0 (2026-05-01) — Sutra now self-executes terminal commands by default. Before this release, Sutra often asked you to run terminal commands yourself; now Sutra runs them itself unless (1) the command needs a real terminal (interactive auth like gcloud auth login, GUI tools, headless-incompatible), (2) the command is on the danger list (force-pushes, recursive deletes outside safe paths, npm/Play Store/App Store publishes, emails, money movement, legal/compliance), or (3) you've marked the command class as always-ask-me. Founder direction D43: "when Sutra asked me to do some terminal things, for Sutra to do those things on its own." Closes the over-handoff pathology — parallel guardrail to ADR-001 Rule 3's OUT-QUERY 3-check (which closes over-asking). New OUT-DIRECT sub-form REQUEST·HUMAN-EXEC joins existing ASK·LATER / HANDOFF / CASCADE. Stage-3 3-check: cant-self-exec (interactive TTY / GUI / OAuth needed, no Bash path), denylist-hit (reuses ADR-001 §4 Rule 4's 6-domain irreversible denylist verbatim — destructive file ops / external sends / founder-reputation outputs / money / legal / irreversible publication), opt-out (command class marked always-founder-runs). NONE hit → demote to Sutra-internal action + OUT-ASSERT INFORM. ANY hit → surface REQUEST·HUMAN-EXEC. Demotion telemetry: 3 new optional fields on the existing turn row in holding/state/interaction/log.jsonl (out_direct_3check_hits / out_direct_demoted / original_out_form) — one-row-per-turn invariant preserved per codex R1 P2 fold. Discipline mode is model-side at emission time in SKILL.md §Stage-3 OUT-DIRECT discipline; classify.sh stays INBOUND-only (ADR-001 invariant preserved). Audit-only post-surface scan deferred to v1.1+ ADR-N. Charter §v1.0 limits: 4 safety guardrails → 5 (codex R2 confirmed this is safety floor not behavior optimization). 2 new fixtures: #14 (demoted-good-case ls -la) + #15 (surfaced-denylist-case git push --force, mirror to #10 across opposite directions). New regression test test-out-direct-3check.sh: 17 assertions including ADR-001 13-fixture backward-compat. All 94 human-sutra tests green pre-commit. Codex convergence: R1 CHANGES-REQUIRED (1 P1 surface gate inconsistency between §1 and §2 / 4 P2: symmetric overstates · demotion telemetry must reuse turn row · PostToolUse hook is wrong layer · denylist must reuse ADR-001 verbatim / 2 P3: skip schema_version · row #10/#15 mirror cases) → all 7 folded → R2 PASS, DIRECTIVE-ID 1777640243. v2.15.1 (2026-05-01) — systemic fix for nudge-skip pattern (founder direction "systemically fix it"). Three preceding releases (v2.14.1 BLUEPRINT-not-showing → v2.15.0 4-discipline parity → this H-Sutra-header-not-showing) all had the same root cause: hook reminder phrased as "(skill: X)" parenthetical, which the model interpreted as "invoke skill X" rather than "emit this text directly." When skill auto-discovery didn't fire for a given turn, the block was silently skipped. v2.15.1 changes phrasing to imperative — "MUST emit literal text" / "MUST invoke skill" — so emission contract is unambiguous. Promotes the H-Sutra header from hardcoded hook-only to canonical schema entry: new `.per_turn_blocks.human_sutra_header` key in sutra-defaults.json (closes the v2.14.1 deferred TODO; eliminates the h-sutra-only-in-hook drift). Hook now reads `.per_turn_blocks.human_sutra_header.format` and `.format_stage_1_fail` instead of hardcoding. Stderr nudge restructured: 7 numbered "MUST emit" lines for the per-turn block stack + 4 "Conditionals (apply when triggered)" lines for codex/skill-explain/readability/right-effort. Asawa side ships in parallel: CLAUDE.md "Mandatory Blocks" gets new H-Sutra Header section above Input Routing + canonical-schema pointer to sutra-defaults.json so future block additions update one place. v2.15.0 (2026-05-01) — governance-parity bump: 3 Asawa-side disciplines reach T4 fleet via expanded per-turn-discipline-prompt.sh stderr emission. Skill-explain card (4-line SKILL/WHAT/WHY/EXPECT/ASKS before invoking any skill, per D40 G3), Readability gate (tables>prose, numbers>adjectives, ASCII boxes for decisions, no unicode boxes — sutra-defaults.json output_discipline keys), and Karpathy right-effort discipline (think first / simpler-alt / surgical scope / verify-loop — promoted from Asawa-only memory to plugin-shipped governance default). Adds new `right_effort` schema key to sutra-defaults.json (Karpathy 4 principles + applies_before + kill_switch). Subagent dispatch briefing (item #2 in audit) was already shipping in v2.14.1 via subagent-dispatch-brief.sh PreToolUse:Task hook — verified emitting 5-block briefing + 4-line footer reminder. Per-turn-discipline-prompt.sh now emits the full block stack (H-Sutra header → IR → Depth → BLUEPRINT → Build-Layer → tool calls → Output Trace) PLUS Codex consult line PLUS Skill-explain reminder PLUS Readability gate reminder PLUS Right-effort principles. Founder direction 2026-05-01: "ship everything to clients" — closes 4 of the v2.14.1 audit gaps (#1 skill-explain, #2 subagent dispatch verify, #3 readability gate, #4 Karpathy). Remaining backlog (v2.16.x): Capability Map (D43) classification gate, Customer Focus First, No-fabrication, Table Shape, PROTO-006. v2.14.1 (2026-05-01) — per-turn-discipline reminder expanded to enumerate ALL 5 per-turn blocks (vinit feedback). v2.14.0 only echoed Input Routing + Depth+Estimation in the stderr nudge; BLUEPRINT, H-Sutra header tag, and OUTPUT TRACE were silently model-side-only with no hook reminder, so T4 fleet (vinit) got the H-Sutra log-write but no visible block-stack discipline. v2.14.1 reads all 5 per_turn_blocks keys from sutra-defaults.json and emits the full block stack in output order: 1. [H-SUTRA HEADER] (first text in response, hardcoded since sutra-defaults.json has no human_sutra section yet — v2.15.0 candidate); 2. INPUT ROUTING; 3. DEPTH + ESTIMATION; 4. BLUEPRINT (when tool calls planned); 5. BUILD-LAYER marker (when editing D38 paths); 6. ... tool calls ...; 7. OUTPUT TRACE one-liner. Plus the existing Codex-consult-at-Depth-≥3 line. Single hook edit (per-turn-discipline-prompt.sh:52-87, +18 net lines), surgical scope per Karpathy. v2.14.0 (2026-05-01) — H-Sutra Layer v1.0 ships to fleet (D42 visibility-before-influence). Folds the H-Sutra classifier (`skills/human-sutra/scripts/classify.sh`) into the existing `hooks/per-turn-discipline-prompt.sh` UserPromptSubmit hook so every turn is classified into the 9-cell CQRS-extended grid (3 verbs DIRECT/QUERY/ASSERT × 3 directions INBOUND/INTERNAL/OUTBOUND) + 3 orthogonal tags (TENSE/TIMING/CHANNEL) + REVERSIBILITY, then appended to `holding/state/interaction/log.jsonl` (Asawa override path) or `.sutra/h-sutra.jsonl` (default). Gap closed: D42 commits (b88b7cc/f65725a/192bea4/a00cda3/7a32af4/106a94a + af84f15 fold) were merged to dev but the marketplace `version` field was stuck at 2.11.1, so cached plugin runtimes never received the H-Sutra logging block — exactly the merged≠shipping anti-pattern D43 ratified hours earlier today. v2.14.0 also catches the marketplace pointer up over the v2.12.0 dispatcher-portability charter (6 Asawa-coupled hooks extracted from plugin to holding/, ~890 lines of dead weight removed from T4 fleet on-disk footprint, codex-converged ADVISORY) and the v2.13.0 python3 removal (vinit#38 escalation, _sutra_project_lib.py retired to archive/2026-05-01-py3-removed-from-bootstrap/, replaced by _sutra_project_lib.sh bash/jq port with identical 4-subcommand surface and identical atomic-write contract; start.sh upfront jq health gate, onboard.sh 4 inline `python3 -c` reads → jq; sandbox-tested with PATH stripped of all python3* — bootstrap rc=0, all 7 fields present, identity preserved). v2.13.0 (2026-05-01) — vinit#38 escalation: remove python3 from /core:start bootstrap entirely. v2.8.11 moved python3 from stdin-heredoc to file-form to dodge SIGKILL from macOS sandbox/EDR agents, but a 2026-05-01 report (@abhishekshah) showed `python3 -c "print('hello')"` itself exits 137 — the binary is killed regardless of invocation form (quarantine xattr, AV process-name killer, codesign mismatch). File-form vs heredoc is irrelevant when python3 can't survive exec. Fix: `_sutra_project_lib.py` retired to archive/2026-05-01-py3-removed-from-bootstrap/, replaced by `_sutra_project_lib.sh` (bash/jq) with identical subcommands (patch-profile / write-onboard / stamp-identity / banner) and identical atomic-write contract (mktemp + mv on same fs; rename(2) atomic). `scripts/start.sh` adds upfront jq health gate with actionable install hint (brew/apt/dnf/source) so missing-jq clients get a clear message rather than silent half-bootstrap. `scripts/onboard.sh` 4 inline `python3 -c` reads (VERSION / EXISTING_OPTIN / FIRST_SEEN / EXISTING_IDENTITY) replaced with jq equivalents; lib calls switched from .py to .sh. Sandbox-tested with PATH stripped of all python3* — bootstrap completes (rc=0), all 7 sutra-project.json fields present, profile/telemetry patch sticks, install_id stable across re-runs, identity preserved. Changes scoped to bootstrap path only (start.sh + onboard.sh + new lib); test/hook python3 callsites left as-is per Karpathy surgical-scope (tests run on dev machines with working python3, not on broken-python3 user machines). v2.8.0 ships codex-sutra v1.0.0 — Sutra-owned codex CLI wrapper at marketplace/plugin/skills/codex-sutra/SKILL.md (540 lines). Three founder-driven changes vs gstack /codex (the prior interim primitive): (1) hard cap raised 5m → 15m to fit high-effort reviews on medium diffs, enforced via wrapper-side polling on a process-group kill (kill -TERM -PGID with setsid / os.setsid fallback) — Bash foreground caps at 10m, so 15m requires bg + polling; (2) fail-closed semantics for non-model failures (codex crash, malformed output, log-write failure, hard-cap timeout) with structured `reason` codes + three-channel result durability (gate-log.jsonl primary, /tmp fallback file, stderr CODEX-SUTRA-RESULT beacon) so PROTO-019 hooks always have an observable verdict; (3) four documented modes — review (P1/P2 gate), challenge (adversarial), consult (with .context/codex-session-id continuity), design-review (single-file review used during this very release for v1→v2→v3 self-review). Skill ships with explicit defense-in-depth (codex sandboxed via -s read-only -C; prompt-level filesystem boundary; output scan post-hoc), single-writer rule for $TMPDONE (eliminates v2 race), stdin closed via </dev/null (codex exec hangs without it — discovered during v2 design review), 24h orphan reaping for /tmp/codex-sutra-* artifacts, and a documented quarterly upstream-sync cadence with gstack /codex. Rollout staged T2 owned (DayFlow/Billu/Paisa/PPR/Maze) week 1 → T3 projects (Testlify/Dharmik) week 2 → T4 fleet week 3+, gated on zero infra-fail verdicts in gate-log.jsonl. v2.8.0 also wires PROTO-019 user-facing messages (codex-directive-gate.sh, codex-review-gate.sh) from /codex to /codex-sutra (5 line changes across 3 hooks) and fixes codex-directive-detect.sh false-positive bug where the regex matched its own task-notification XML echoes — new perl-based system-XML strip step (allowlist: task-notification, system-reminder, command-name, command-message, command-args, local-command-stdout, local-command-stderr) with graceful fallback when perl unavailable. 6/6 unit tests pass; pre-existing official test suite has v2-vs-v3 marker-path rot (2026-04-25 transition) tracked separately. Codex review chain for design: v1 CHANGES-REQUIRED → v2 CHANGES-REQUIRED → v3 ADVISORY → ship; for hooks: v1 CHANGES-REQUIRED → v2 PASS → delta PASS. Verdict files at .enforcement/codex-reviews/2026-04-28-*. v2.7.1 actually applies the assistant-observer.sh B7+B9 fix that v2.7.0's CURRENT-VERSION.md described as shipped but whose code change was missed during the v2.7.0 commit. The narrative in the v2.6.3/v2.7.0 sections was forward-referencing a working-tree edit that never made it into the commit. v2.7.1 closes the gap: drops the redundant nested ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} from the HOOK_LOG default value (closing B7 path-double-substitution + B9 set-u crash with one edit), and switches the event evidence_refs[0].source field from `${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/holding/hooks/hook-log.jsonl` to `$HOOK_LOG` so the SUTRA_ASSISTANT_HOOK_LOG override is reflected accurately. Mirrored to holding/hooks/assistant-observer.sh in the holding repo. 24/24 holding/hooks/tests/test-assistant-observer.sh tests green. v2.7.0 streamlines the permission system per codex consult 2026-04-28: removes ~50% of code (1366 → 690 LoC) without changing threat model or behavior. DELETED: lib/sh_lex_check.py (228 LoC v2.4 compositional matcher — Trust Mode is a strict superset for Bash); scripts/rollback-compositional.sh; 3 obsolete test files; _match_bash_compositional + _env_has_shadowing in permission-gate.sh (~50 LoC); allowlist mirror + env-shadowing + Haiku LLM path in bash-summary-pretool.sh (~210 LoC). REFACTORED: bash-summary-pretool.sh is now a 152-LoC thin wrapper that calls sh_trust_mode.py once and emits one of 6 deterministic category labels (no LLM cost, equivalent UX). Single source of truth: every hook calls the same classifier, eliminating drift bugs codex caught (rtk * and claude plugin update * skipped by bash-summary but not by permission-gate). Tier 1 retained for Write/Edit/MultiEdit + plugin-lifecycle Bash per codex correction. 156/156 trust-mode unit tests green. Threat model unchanged (single trusted local operator). v2.6.3 closes B7+B9 in assistant-observer.sh (HOOK_LOG line) — single edit fixes both. B7: prior `${SUTRA_ASSISTANT_HOOK_LOG:-$REPO_ROOT/${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/holding/hooks/hook-log.jsonl}` double-substituted REPO_ROOT and an absolute CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR producing an invalid path that silently no-op'd. B9 (discovered 2026-04-28 verifying v2.6.2 deployed shim): same line crashed under `set -uo pipefail` when CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR unset (manual-shell opt-in path). Fix: drop the redundant nested CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR — REPO_ROOT alone is the correct base; HOOK_LOG override path preserved. Event src field switched to `$HOOK_LOG` for accuracy when SUTRA_ASSISTANT_HOOK_LOG override is used. Mirrored to holding/hooks/assistant-observer.sh. Re-enable pre-conditions (assistant layer, paused per D37): B1, B5, B6, B8 remain — B2/B3/B4 fixed in v2.6.2, B7+B9 fixed here. v2.6.2 ships D36+D37 (founder directives 2026-04-27, decoupled by mechanism). D36 Feedback Channel three-layer defense after a real client session bypassed /core:feedback --public via direct `gh issue create` when the binary failed on a non-existent `feedback,vunknown` label: (a) routing-rule.sh strengthened with explicit no-gh-fallback clause during binary error recovery; (b) NEW feedback-channel-guard.sh PreToolUse:Bash hook blocks `gh (issue|pr) (create|comment|review)` against any sankalpasawa/sutra* repo with exit 2 (12/12 tests; SUTRA_FEEDBACK_GUARD_DISABLED=1 kill-switch); (c) feedback.sh binary --label flag dropped, version moved to title `[feedback v${PLUGIN_VERSION}] from plugin`. D37 Sutra Assistant Layer: paused, default-OFF — polarity flipped in assistant-kill-switch.sh, opt-in via ~/.sutra-assistant-enabled or SUTRA_ASSISTANT_ENABLED; hard kill-switches still win. bin/sutra `enable`/`disable` rewritten. Bug list B1-B8 captured in holding/research/2026-04-24-assistant-layer-design.md §0 — re-enable requires clearing those before `sutra enable`. v2.6.1 refines Trust Mode `gh`/`git` to a catastrophic-only rule per founder direction ("keep it simple — only delete"): `gh` removed from REMOTE_TOOLS, prompts only on delete-class actions (`gh repo delete`, `gh secret delete`/`remove`, `gh release delete`, `gh issue delete`, `gh codespace delete`, `gh label delete`, `gh extension remove`, `gh gist delete`, `gh variable delete`); `git` simplified to prompt only on `push --force`/`-f`/`--force-with-lease` and `clean -f*`. Everything else (gh pr create/edit/merge/close/comment, issue create/comment, repo create/edit/fork, secret set, auth login/logout, workflow run, gh api any-method; git commit/push/pull/rebase/merge/reset --hard/checkout/branch -D/tag -d/stash drop/rm/mv) auto-approves — all recoverable via reflog, remote, or fs. 156/156 unit tests green. Supersedes the initial v2.6.1 granular spec (36431a8) per 16af031. v2.6.0 ships PROTO-024 V1 client→team feedback fanout: /core:feedback now scrubs locally and pushes to sankalpasawa/sutra-data clients/<install_id>/feedback/ (collaborator-visible inbox, V2 will encrypt). Strengthened scrub_text() with 12+ token detectors (GitHub/OpenAI/AWS/Slack/Stripe/JWT/signed-URL/DSN/webhook/email/phone) plus 40+ char high-entropy fallback. Decoupled /core:feedback from SUTRA_TELEMETRY=0. push.sh stops writing manifest.identity (closes v1.9 PII leak). reset-turn-markers.sh moved from UserPromptSubmit→Stop event (closes spoof vulnerability — no content pattern detection needed). Codex review 2026-04-25 directive 1777062127+1777058308. Governance preserved end-to-end. v2.5.0 adds Tier 1.6 Trust Mode (denylist of 6 prompt categories: git mutations, privilege, recursive delete outside safe paths, disk/system, fetch-and-exec, remote/shared-state). Auto-approves everything else. Per founder approval-fatigue feedback + Codex MODIFY->GO. Operating system for building with AI. v2.1.1 patch (fleet feedback + codex review 2026-04-25): (a) /core:start project-root guard — refuses activation in $HOME or non-project dirs (no .git / package.json / pyproject.toml / Cargo.toml / go.mod / CLAUDE.md), canonical path compare prevents symlink/trailing-slash bypass, .git-as-file recognized for worktrees/submodules. --force override. Prevents home-dir poisoning of ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md with project-scoped governance. Covered by tests/unit/test-start-guard.sh (10 assertions). (b) /core:feedback docs synced to v2.1 reality — --public flag to gh CLI documented (was already wired but commands/feedback.md + usage help still said 'NOT YET IMPLEMENTED'). (c) hooks/reset-turn-markers.sh: root-cause fix — treats empty PROMPT as synthetic turn (was falling through to wipe, caused 2052:1 wipe:skip ratio blocking multi-tool Sutra edits). v2.0 — privacy model replaced: signals-not-content capture, consent-gated disk writes, T4 in-memory-default. User surface = single PRIVACY.md sheet + SUTRA_TELEMETRY=0 opt-out + /sutra feedback manual channel. Legacy v1.9 identity stamping + outbound push DEPRECATED, gated behind SUTRA_LEGACY_TELEMETRY=1. Governance surface preserved: input routing, depth estimation, readability gate, output trace, session-retrieve, per-profile enforcement, C3c token-compression, permission-gate, Bash prompt summarizer, feedback-routing-rule. Codex-reviewed DIRECTIVE-ID 1777065370 (CHANGES-REQUIRED → all P1/P2 findings absorbed).

profile.title: Enforcement profile
profile.description: individual = warn-only, telemetry off. project = warn-only, telemetry on (default). company = HARD block on missing depth markers, telemetry on.
